cards.title: AI Glossary: Essential Terms for 2026
cards.body: TechCrunch compiles a comprehensive glossary of crucial AI terminology and slang. A must-read guide for navigating the rapidly evolving AI landscape.

cards.title: Browser Wars: Chrome and Safari Alternatives
cards.body: TechCrunch reviews top alternative browsers challenging Chrome and Safari's dominance. Explore new options reshaping the browser landscape.

cards.title: Zuckerberg: AI Progress Slower Than Expected
cards.body: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg told staff that AI agent development hasn't accelerated as anticipated. Internal discussions reveal concerns about development timelines.

cards.title: Jersey Mike's IPO Exposes AI Hype Bubble
cards.body: The sandwich chain's IPO documents mention AI despite having no obvious need for it. A stark example of how pervasive AI marketing has become.

cards.title: Meta Launches Pocket: AI Gaming App
cards.body: Meta quietly released Pocket, an experimental app for generating interactive mini-games via text prompts. Users can create and share AI-powered gaming experiences.

cards.title: Anthropic Discusses Custom Chip with Samsung
cards.body: Anthropic is in talks with Samsung about developing custom AI chips. The move mirrors OpenAI's recent announcement of a Broadcom partnership.

cards.title: OpenAI Proposes 5% Equity to Sovereign Fund
cards.body: Sam Altman proposed donating 5% of OpenAI's equity to a U.S. sovereign wealth fund. The plan would let the public benefit from AI boom gains.

cards.title: Microsoft Launches AI Deployment Company
cards.body: Microsoft commits $2.5 billion to a new AI deployment group. The company joins Amazon, OpenAI, and Anthropic in building infrastructure.

cards.title: OpenClaw Automates Dating with AI Scripts
cards.body: Ben Guez created an automated script using OpenClaw and Claude to manage dating interactions. The tool reportedly generated significant international interest.

cards.title: Indian Billionaire Invests $30M in Office AI
cards.body: Bhavin Turakhia's startup Neo challenges Microsoft Office and Google Apps with AI. His fifth venture targets enterprise software with $30 million commitment.

cards.title: SpaceX Reveals AI Device Prototype to Investors
cards.body: SpaceX showcased a handset-like AI device before its IPO roadshow. The prototype signals SpaceX's interest in expanding into wireless markets.

cards.title: Ashton Kutcher Launches Infrastructure-Focused VC Firm
cards.body: Kutcher and Morgan Beller founded a new VC firm focusing on AI infrastructure and energy. The move diverges from Sound Ventures' lab-focused strategy.

cards.title: Cloudflare Demands AI Companies Pay for Content
cards.body: Cloudflare requires AI companies to separate search crawlers from training crawlers by September 15. Non-compliance risks default blocking on publisher sites.

cards.title: Venice AI Reaches Unicorn Status with $65M
cards.body: Venice AI secured $65M Series A funding while achieving profitability. The privacy-first platform generates over $70M annualized revenue.

cards.title: Google's Gemini Spark Arrives on Mac
cards.body: Gemini Spark, Google's agentic assistant, now available on Mac with expanded features. Real-time tracking and broader app support included.

cards.title: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026: Builders Stage Unveiled
cards.body: Builders Stage returns to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 with 10,000+ founders and investors. Register now and save up to $330 on tickets.

cards.title: Meta Monetizes AI Compute Like SpaceX
cards.body: Meta develops cloud infrastructure business selling AI compute and model access. The move directly competes with AWS, Google Cloud, and Azure.

cards.title: Vinton Cerf Retires as Google's Internet Evangelist
cards.body: Internet protocol co-creator Vinton Cerf steps down from Google next week. The Father of the Internet concludes his chief evangelist role.

cards.title: Trump Drops Restrictions on Anthropic Models
cards.body: Trump administration removed restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos and Fable models. The erratic policy approach leaves industry stakeholders uncertain about future regulations.

cards.title: Wayve Launches $85M Employee Tender Offer
cards.body: Wayve values the company at $8.5B through employee tender offering. The move reflects growing AI startup trend of using tenders for retention.

cards.title: The only AI glossary you'll need this year
cards.body: The AI boom has created an avalanche of new terminology and industry jargon. TechCrunch compiled a comprehensive glossary defining essential AI words and phrases you're likely to encounter.

cards.title: Mark Zuckerberg: AI agents progressing slower than hoped
cards.body: Meta's CEO told staff that AI development efforts are not moving as quickly as anticipated. The admission reveals challenges in advancing AI agent technology at scale.

cards.title: Meta launches Pocket: vibe-coded AI gaming app
cards.body: Meta quietly released Pocket, an experimental AI application enabling users to generate and share interactive mini games using text prompts. The app showcases Meta's push into generative AI consumer products.

cards.title: Anthropic discussing custom chip with Samsung
cards.body: Anthropic is in talks with Samsung to develop a proprietary AI chip, mirroring OpenAI's recent announcement of a custom chip partnership with Broadcom. This reflects the industry's shift toward specialized hardware for AI workloads.

cards.title: OpenAI proposes donating 5% equity to US fund
cards.body: Sam Altman proposed giving 5% of OpenAI's equity to a U.S. sovereign wealth fund, reviving public discourse about shared financial gains from the AI boom. The move could reshape how AI companies structure ownership.

cards.title: Microsoft launches AI deployment company
cards.body: Microsoft committed $2.5 billion to establish its own AI deployment group, joining Amazon, OpenAI, and Anthropic in the infrastructure race. The move signals intensifying competition in enterprise AI infrastructure.

cards.title: Indian tech tycoon bets $30M on AI Office alternative
cards.body: Bhavin Turakhia's new venture Neo challenges Microsoft Office and Google Apps with AI-powered enterprise software. The startup represents a growing trend of AI-native productivity tools.

cards.title: SpaceX develops AI handset-like device prototype
cards.body: SpaceX showed investors a prototype of a 'handset-like' AI device before going public. The device hints at SpaceX's potential expansion into wireless communications.

cards.title: Ashton Kutcher launches new VC firm for AI infrastructure
cards.body: Kutcher and Morgan Beller launched a new venture fund focused on AI infrastructure and energy rather than AI labs themselves. The fund targets the foundational layer powering leading AI companies.

cards.title: Cloudflare requires AI companies to pay for content
cards.body: Cloudflare mandated that AI companies separate web crawlers for search from those for AI training by September 15 or face blocking. The policy aims to protect publishers from unauthorized AI model training.

cards.title: Venice AI reaches unicorn status with $65M Series A
cards.body: Privacy-first AI platform Venice AI became a unicorn, already boasting $70M+ annualized revenue. The milestone reflects growing demand for privacy-conscious AI solutions.

cards.title: Google's Gemini Spark now available on Mac
cards.body: Google expanded its 24/7 agentic assistant Gemini Spark to macOS with real-time tracking and broader app support. The rollout continues Google's push to embed AI agents across devices.

cards.title: Meta converts excess AI compute into cloud business
cards.body: Meta is developing a cloud infrastructure business to monetize excess AI compute capacity, competing with AWS, Google Cloud, and Azure. The move represents a new revenue stream from Meta's massive AI investments.

cards.title: Trump drops restrictions on Anthropic AI models
cards.body: The Trump administration lifted restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos and Fable models, reflecting inconsistent AI policy. Companies face ongoing uncertainty about future regulatory guidance.

cards.title: Wayve launches $85M employee tender offer
cards.body: Autonomous driving AI startup Wayve offered employees tender opportunities at an $8.5B valuation. The move reflects a growing trend of AI startups using tenders to retain talent.

cards.title: Achieving operational excellence with AI
cards.body: AI is enabling companies to apply frameworks like Lean Six Sigma and business process management with new precision at scale. Modern AI tools promise structured approaches to operational complexity.

cards.title: Teaching AI to run with the turbines
cards.body: AI's most consequential applications operate far from consumer chatbots—powering critical industrial infrastructure and operations. Industries relying on physical assets and safety are increasingly deploying AI for optimization.

cards.title: LLMs stuck in groupthink groove, startup intervenes
cards.body: A new startup is working to solve language model groupthink, where AI systems produce suspiciously similar outputs. The startup's solution aims to increase diversity and independence in LLM responses.

cards.title: Claude Science: Anthropic's research flagship
cards.body: Anthropic announced Claude Science, designed to support scientific research similarly to how Claude Code aids software engineering. The tool can autonomously assist researchers in advancing pharmaceutical and biotech work.

cards.title: Google's AI buildout drove 37% electricity increase
cards.body: Google's massive AI infrastructure expansion caused a 37% surge in electricity consumption during 2025. The energy demands of large-scale AI deployment are becoming a significant sustainability concern.

cards.title: Trump gets OpenAI stake far lower than proposed
cards.body: The Trump administration secured a 5% OpenAI equity stake, significantly lower than Senator Sanders' target amount. The deal reflects ongoing negotiations between government and AI companies over public benefit structures.
